# Transcrição: 14-Group Coaching Basics

- **Fonte:** 14-Group Coaching Basics
- **Duração:** 07:07

## [00:00 - 03:04]

You ever been stuck on a flight and crammed into a middle seat like a sardine and you just had to sit there for hours and hours on end and you just wished that you had a first class seat but you didn't. Nope, you're stuck in the back. You're stuck in the back and you're crammed in there. Like a little fish in a can. Well that's what it can start to feel like when you have too many private clients and a lot of people say that coaching is not a scalable business but that is because they don't have group coaching. Group coaching is amazing and if I could turn back time I would have added group coaching from day one. Now that's a controversial thing to say but stick with me. Just imagine you're on that same flight but this time instead of you being crammed like a little sardine in the back, you're still in the back of the plane but you've got all this wing room because your neighbors didn't make the flight. They didn't bother to buy a seat or whatever happened. You still had that nice cozy economy priced seat but you have lots of space. That's what happens to your calendar when everybody's hopping onto the same spot. That's group coaching. Group coaching, everybody is coming onto the same time slot to get your amazing coaching. Now for years I didn't set up group coaching because I thought that being a premium coach meant that I had to give personalized private time to everyone or else I was being cheap. Here's the thing, a lot of people, a lot of people, not everyone, a lot of people are craving community. So they are happy to jump onto a group coaching time slot to yes get your amazing coaching, get your insights, get your curriculum and have all of that same experience. They will pay maybe a little less than your private clients would but you get to sell the same hour. And that is how your coaching business becomes a scalable enterprise, how your time ends up giving you a lot more wing room. And I was really surprised when I started to do this at how impactful it was for so many of my clients who were able to make meaningful progress because they had community. I completely underestimated the value of community and there were a lot of people who craved the benefit of coaching who wanted in on coaching but they didn't want to invest

## [03:04 - 06:05]

at the level of private coaching. It gave them an accessible option. So yeah, I got some wing room, they got some wing room, nobody had to pay the first class prices, it's a win-win situation. Now there's some big myths in the coaching industry about when you're so-called ready to start doing group coaching and I want to bust those myths right now. You know, there's no coachy police that are going to stop you from doing group coaching right away. A lot of times people say, oh, you're going to have to do private coaching for a certain amount of time, a certain number of clients and then when you're ready then you can do group coaching or you've got to have a certain number of followers or a certain size of a list before you start doing your group coaching. A lot of that has to do with whether you feel comfortable inviting people into a group situation. Group coaching does feel different and is facilitated differently than private coaching. It's a slightly different experience. You're still asking questions, you're still facilitating along the curriculum. The difference though is you're facilitating in a group of people versus having a one-on-one confidential experience. You can be an amazing group coach, but the longer you defer on starting your group coaching experience, the longer you wait to start getting good at it. I wish I started my group coaching so much earlier because I wanted to offer something that people could say yes to sooner, that I could multiply my time in sooner and that would be so beneficial to so many people who really wanted the power of community in addition to my powerful curriculum-based coaching. The truth is a lot of people don't do it because they don't want to do the marketing and they don't want to do the selling. The thing is, you've got an amazing secret up your sleeve, don't you? Because you're doing this program that's teaching you everything you need to know with the marketing and the sales part. You really don't have that challenge. I would really strongly encourage you to consider starting your group coaching as soon as you're comfortable to do it. If you're like, hey, what if I only have one person in role in my group coaching? Here's how you do it. Pick a time that you're going to do your group coaching one time per week for the consecutive number of weeks and you take your curriculum and you can go to Gigi and ask how it might change based on being a group curriculum versus a private curriculum. You're probably going to find it's not all that different. But then commit to doing that time slot every single week for the rest of the curriculum. Then commit to selling it.

## [06:05 - 07:07]
